text:
Market today: Mild risk-off mood after jobs came in weak (57K payrolls, unemployment 4.2%) - that's the real driver today. Soft labor cools rate-cut hopes but also caps yields, so tech names are in a tug-of-war. No major catalyst directly hitting chips or software today.

Your holdings: CRWD is the one screaming for attention - you're down 72.33% and your stop is listed at 590, which looks like a data error vs. the $199 price. Clarify that stop immediately; on margin, a position this far underwater is a live grenade. SOXL is down 12.8% and SPAL down 13.2% - both leveraged, both decaying, both need tight stops respected ($200 and $21.38). Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: 1) Clarify and fix the CRWD stop - that $590 figure makes no sense at current price. 2) Watch how tech digests the weak jobs print as the session develops.

Bottom line: Fix the CRWD stop now, then hold your other names tight - no reason to add risk into a soft macro day.
plays: CRWD: Your stop at $590 vs. a $199 price is almost certainly a data error - find your real exit level today before anything else. This is your biggest risk on margin.
SOXL and SPAL: Both leveraged, both down hard, both have stops close by ($200 and $21.38 respectively) - respect those stops if hit, no averaging down.
No new buys today - weak jobs print + margin exposure already elevated means sitting on hands is the right call.
